reforms.description: A new regulation was adopted in June 2015 to simplify bureaucratic procedures when applying for R&D fiscal incentives, aiming to promote the use of the research and development tax credit among younger firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying access to R&D tax credits encourages more firms to invest in research and development, thereby enhancing innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: A new regulation was adopted to simplify procedures for R&D tax credits.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly improves access to R&D tax credits, which can stimulate innovation among younger firms, but does not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: A new regulation was adopted in June 2015 to simplify bureaucratic procedures when applying for R&D fiscal incentives.

reforms.description: Spain's government provides dedicated public financing for innovation investments in the business sector through R&D tax credits and direct funding programmes. However, the complexity of the procedures has led to low uptake among firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages firms to invest in R&D and innovation, which can enhance productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The state provides dedicated public financing for innovation investments.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses significant barriers to innovation financing, aiming to improve the effectiveness of existing support mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: The state provides dedicated public financing for innovation investments in the business sector via R&D tax credits and government direct funding programmes.

reforms.description: The public national innovation company (ENISA) provides funding for early-stage start-ups, allowing them to establish a track record to attract private venture capital investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates the growth of innovative start-ups, which can contribute to job creation and economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: ENISA helps to finance early-stage start-ups, allowing them to establish a long enough track record to convince private venture capitalists to invest.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform supports early-stage firms that are often underserved by traditional financing, enhancing the overall start-up ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The public national innovation company (ENISA) helps to finance early-stage start-ups, allowing them to establish a long enough track record to convince private venture capitalists to invest.

reforms.description: The 'Innvierte' programme of the Centro de Desarrollo Tecnológico Industrial (CDTI) aims to promote business innovation by supporting venture capital investment in technology-based and innovative companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages private investment in innovative firms, which can lead to increased technological advancement and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The 'Innvierte' programme aims to promote business innovation by supporting venture capital investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme plays a significant role in enhancing the funding landscape for innovative firms, fostering collaboration between public and private sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The 'Innvierte' programme of CDTI aims to promote business innovation by supporting venture capital investment in technology-based and innovative companies.

reforms.description: Law 5/2015 includes a series of measures to facilitate SMEs' access to banking credit in the short term, establishing regulations on crowd-funding and revising the regulatory framework of securitisations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating access to credit for SMEs enhances their ability to invest in innovation and growth, which can lead to increased productivity and economic expansion.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was enacted to improve SMEs' access to banking credit.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it broadens financing options for SMEs, which are crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Law 5/2015 includes a series of measures to facilitate SMEs' access to banking credit in the short term.

reforms.description: The Spanish government initiated a reform in 2015 to modernize and internationalize university education, aiming to enhance cooperation with the business sector and adapt study programs to labor market needs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning educational outcomes with labor market needs, the reform is expected to enhance the employability of graduates and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was enacted to improve university education and its alignment with the labor market.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the education system, aiming to better integrate higher education with the needs of the economy, which is crucial for long-term growth.
reforms.source_quote: A 2015 Royal Decree aims at making study programmes and funding more flexible and to enhance co-operation with the business sector to adapt programmes to labour market needs.

reforms.description: A new training-for-employment model was adopted in September 2015, focusing on transparency, competition, and quality assessment in public training management.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the quality and relevance of training, the reform aims to better match skills supply with labor market demand, thus enhancing productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The new model was adopted to enhance the management of public training resources.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform introduces significant changes to the training system, aiming to improve the quality and relevance of vocational training, which is important for addressing skill mismatches.
reforms.source_quote: The new training-for-employment model, adopted in September 2015, is based on the principle of greater transparency and control in the management of public resources.

reforms.description: The Law on the Promotion of Business Financing adopted in 2015 aims to improve access to bank credit for SMEs and develop alternatives to bank funding, including measures to strengthen the position of SMEs towards banks and improve mutual guarantee funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances access to financing for SMEs, which can lead to increased innovation and growth in the startup ecosystem.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was adopted and is in effect to improve SME financing.
reforms.importance_rationale: This law represents a significant structural change in how SMEs access financing, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government has implemented the Law on the Promotion Business Financing adopted in April 2015, devoted to improving access to bank credit and developing non-bank financial intermediation.

reforms.description: Spain aims to improve the allocation of capital to innovative firms by enhancing bank and capital market financing, reallocating public financing to early-stage finance, and shifting some funding from loans to grants for R&D projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to finance for innovative firms, the reform is expected to enhance productivity and stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy outlines priorities for fostering innovative business investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how public financing is allocated to support innovation, addressing chronic low productivity growth in Spain.
reforms.source_quote: To foster a better allocation of capital, a first priority is to better tune bank, capital market and government financing to the needs of new innovative firms.

reforms.description: Spain's investment in knowledge-based capital (KBC) remains low, significantly below levels in countries with stronger productivity growth, indicating a need for policies to enhance investment in this area.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing investment in KBC is linked to higher productivity and growth, which can enhance overall economic performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The text indicates a need for improvement but does not specify if reforms have been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: Addressing the low investment in KBC is significant for enhancing productivity growth, but it is part of a broader set of challenges in the investment landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The level of investment in knowledge-based capital (KBC) remains low... less than half than in countries such as the United States and France.

reforms.description: Spain's government is prioritizing seed capital and early-stage financing for small innovative start-ups that typically rely on intangible assets, addressing the gap in private financing for these firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing necessary funding to innovative start-ups, the initiative supports job creation and technological advancement, which can enhance overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has stated its intention to prioritize seed capital for innovative start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant gap in financing for innovative firms, which is crucial for fostering a vibrant startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Government programs to support finance to firms should prioritize providing seed capital and early-stage financing of the small innovative start-ups.

reforms.description: The CDTI Innvierte programme provides venture capital and seed capital to innovative companies, aiming to enhance their technological capabilities and internationalization.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to venture capital, the programme supports the growth of innovative firms, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The CDTI is set to provide venture capital and seed capital to innovative companies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it directly supports the funding needs of innovative firms, which are critical for economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: The CDTI Innvierte programme provides venture capital and seed capital together with private investors.

reforms.description: The Spanish government plans to shift funds available via loans to a direct grant fund, where funds to firms and researchers would be granted based on performance and international peer review to increase firms’ R&D and slow the brain drain of talented researchers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing direct grants based on performance, the reform incentivizes R&D investment, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government should shift funds available via loans to a direct grant fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding strategy that could fundamentally change how R&D is financed in Spain, enhancing the innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The government should shift funds available via loans to a direct grant fund, where funds to firms and researchers would be granted based on performance and international peer review.

reforms.description: The Spanish government has introduced financial education in the school curriculum and aims to improve current entrepreneurs’ financial literacy through specific programmes such as training, mentoring, and coaching.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving financial literacy among entrepreneurs can enhance their ability to manage resources effectively, leading to better business outcomes and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Spain has recently introduced financial education in the school curriculum, with positive results.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform builds on existing educational frameworks and aims to enhance the skills of entrepreneurs, which is important for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Spain has recently introduced financial education in the school curriculum, with positive results.

reforms.description: Spain aims to increase public and private funding for innovative firms during the seed and early start-up phases, enhancing their capacity to innovate and grow.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for innovative firms is expected to enhance their research capabilities and market competitiveness, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to increase funding was made as part of the broader strategy to foster innovative business investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding strategy aimed at supporting early-stage innovative firms, which is crucial for enhancing the overall innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Increase public and private funding for innovative firms at the seed and early start-up phases.
